Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»OLYMPIC MEMORY: Chad le Clos did THIS 12 years ago today in London

Chad Le Clos is SA’s most decorated Olympian of all time with four medals – and he’ll be out to add to that tally in Paris this year.

Chad Le Closis South Africa’s most decorated Olympian of all time withfourmedals to his name – and he’ll be out to add to that tally in the pool in Paris in the coming days.

Enjoy the Paris Olympic Games 2024 experience at your favourite ATKV Resort!

Le Clos, now 32, joined an exclusive club of South Africans at the 2024 Paris Olympics when he was named in the Team SA squad for hisfourthOlympics.

He joins Khotso Mokoena, Ryk Neethling, Hendrik Ramaala, Roland Schoeman and Sunette Viljoen as four-time South African Olympians.

For the latest Paris Olympics 2024 news, bookmarkThe South African website’sdedicated section for free-to-read content

The most memorable of Le Clos’ medals came when he out-touched American superstarMichael Phelpsto win the men’s 200m butterfly gold medalON THIS DAYin London in 2012

Durban-born Le Clos hit the wall in a time of 1:52.96 with Phelps 5/100th of a second behind in 1:53.01.

Japan’s Takeshi Matsuda took the bronze in 1:53.21.

In addition to that gold medal, Le Clos also won silver in the men’s 100m butterfly in both London and Rio de Janeiro in 2016, and took the runner-up spot in the 200m freestyle in Brazil.

In aflashbackto that day on London, the official OlympicsX pageposted the video clip showing the closing seconds of that 200m butterfly duel between Le Clos and Phelps – as well as a heart-warming message from Chad le Clos’ father, Bert.

Meanwhile, Le Clos has qualified for the men’s 100m butterfly in Paris as part of an eight-strong Team SA aquatics squad.

However, as reported byThe South African website, Le Clos is battling an arm injury.

Le Clos is considered a rank outsider for a medal – or to even make the final – and is currently ranked outside the top 30 athletes in the world this season.

He will swim in thesecond of the five heatson Friday, 2 August at11:03(SA time).

CompatriotMatthew Sateshas been drawn in the same heat.

The top 16 swimmers will progress to the semi-finals later in the day at21:00(SA time).

The final is scheduled for Saturday, 3 August at20:30(SA time).

Good luck Chad and Matthew!

As a reminder, below are the financial incentives promised by theSouth African Sports Confederation and Olympic Committee (Sascoc).

IndividualsGold medallists R400 000 and the coach R100 000Silver medallists R200 000 and the coach R50 000Bronze medallists R75 000 and the coach R25 000

In addition, as reported byThe South African website, new Minister of Sport, Arts and Culture,Gayton McKenzie, promised that he has a ‘big surprise’ for all Team SA medal winners, suggesting there could be more prize money coming their way.

Men:Pieter Coetze (100m, 200m backstroke), Chad le Clos (100m butterfly), Matthew Sates (100m, 200m butterfly and 200m IM)

Women:Aimee Canny (200m freestyle), Kaylene Corbett (200m breaststroke), Erin Gallagher (100m butterfly), Rebecca Meder (200m IM), Tatjana Smith (100m, 200m breaststroke)
